questions.q: In which Indian state are the Ellora Caves located?
questions.explanation: The Ellora Caves are in Maharashtra, near the city of Aurangabad (Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar).

questions.q: What type of monument is Ellora?
questions.explanation: Ellora is a complex of rock-cut caves and temples carved into a basalt cliff.

questions.q: The most famous monument at Ellora is the temple dedicated to which Hindu deity?
questions.explanation: The Kailasa temple at Ellora is dedicated to Lord Shiva and represents Mount Kailash.

questions.q: What is the name of Ellora's colossal rock-cut temple?
questions.explanation: The Kailasa (Kailasanatha) temple is Ellora's most celebrated monument.

questions.q: What international status does Ellora hold?
questions.explanation: Ellora was inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1983.

questions.q: The caves at Ellora represent how many different religions?
questions.explanation: Ellora's caves belong to three faiths: Buddhism, Hinduism and Jainism.

questions.q: Which three religions are represented among the Ellora caves?
questions.explanation: Ellora uniquely combines Buddhist, Hindu and Jain rock-cut monuments at one site.

questions.q: The Kailasa temple was carved out of what kind of material?
questions.explanation: The Kailasa temple was carved from a single massive rock of the basalt cliff.

questions.q: How were the Ellora monuments created?
questions.explanation: The monuments were excavated and sculpted directly out of the living rock of the hillside.

questions.q: Ellora is located near which other famous group of rock-cut caves in Maharashtra?
questions.explanation: The Ajanta Caves lie not far from Ellora in the same region of Maharashtra.

questions.q: Which royal dynasty is credited with commissioning the great Kailasa temple at Ellora?
questions.explanation: The Kailasa temple was commissioned under the Rashtrakuta dynasty.

questions.q: The Kailasa temple is traditionally attributed to which Rashtrakuta king?
questions.explanation: The Kailasa temple is generally credited to the Rashtrakuta king Krishna I in the 8th century.

questions.q: In roughly which century was the Kailasa temple created?
questions.explanation: The Kailasa temple was carved in the 8th century CE under Rashtrakuta patronage.

questions.q: What is especially remarkable about how the Kailasa temple was carved?
options: It was carved from the top downward
It was carved from the bottom upward
It was carved horizontally into a plain
questions.explanation: The Kailasa temple was excavated top-down, beginning at the roof and working downward into the rock.

questions.q: Approximately how many major caves make up the Ellora complex?
questions.explanation: Ellora comprises roughly 34 major rock-cut caves across the Buddhist, Hindu and Jain groups.

questions.q: Which mountain is the Kailasa temple designed to symbolically represent?
questions.explanation: The Kailasa temple represents Mount Kailash, the Himalayan abode of Lord Shiva.

questions.q: Cave 16, the Kailasa temple, is famous for being the world's largest example of what?
questions.explanation: The Kailasa temple is considered the largest single monolithic rock excavation in the world.

questions.q: The Ellora caves are carved into which type of volcanic rock?
questions.explanation: Ellora is carved into the basalt of the Charanandri (Deccan) hills.

questions.q: Why is Ellora often cited as a symbol of religious harmony in ancient India?
options: It was built by a single religious order
Buddhist, Hindu and Jain shrines coexist side by side
It was never used for worship
It blends Islamic and Christian art
questions.explanation: Monuments of three faiths were created next to one another, reflecting the era's religious tolerance.

questions.q: Ellora lies along an important historic route connecting the Deccan interior to which region?
options: The western seaports of the Deccan
questions.explanation: Ellora sat on an ancient trade route linking the Deccan to the western coastal ports, aiding its prosperity.

questions.q: The Buddhist group at Ellora includes a famous multi-storey prayer (chaitya) hall known as the 'Carpenter's Cave.' What is its other name?
questions.explanation: Cave 10, the Vishvakarma Cave, is a Buddhist chaitya hall nicknamed the Carpenter's Cave for its ribbed, timber-like ceiling.

questions.q: Which group of Ellora caves features the elaborate shrine known as the Indra Sabha?
questions.explanation: The Indra Sabha is the most elaborate of Ellora's Jain caves, richly carved in the later phase of the site.

questions.q: An estimated how much rock was removed to create the Kailasa temple?
questions.explanation: Excavating the Kailasa temple is estimated to have removed on the order of 200,000 tonnes of rock.

questions.q: The Kailasa temple is identified as which numbered cave at Ellora?
questions.explanation: The Kailasa temple is designated Cave 16 in the Ellora numbering system.

questions.q: The Kailasa temple's architectural style most closely follows which south Indian temple tradition?
questions.explanation: The Kailasa temple was modelled on the Dravidian (south Indian) style of temple architecture.

questions.q: A striking sculpted panel at the Kailasa temple depicts which demon-king shaking Mount Kailash?
questions.explanation: A celebrated relief shows the demon-king Ravana attempting to lift and shake Mount Kailash beneath Shiva.

questions.q: How many overall phases of construction are generally recognized across the three religious groups at Ellora?
questions.explanation: Ellora's caves were developed across roughly three broad phases corresponding to its Buddhist, Hindu and Jain monuments.

questions.q: The modern city closest to Ellora, long used as its access point, has historically been called what?
questions.explanation: Ellora lies near Aurangabad (now also known as Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar) in Maharashtra.

questions.q: Within the Hindu group, Cave 21 at Ellora is known by which name?
questions.explanation: Cave 21, the Rameshwar Cave, is a Hindu cave noted for its finely carved river-goddess and amorous couple figures.

questions.q: The Rashtrakuta dynasty that patronized Ellora had its early power base in which part of India?
questions.explanation: The Rashtrakutas ruled from the Deccan plateau, the region where Ellora itself is situated.
